schemes.label: ECDSA-STARK (baseline)
schemes.notes: Classical control. Shor-breakable; not a PQ candidate. Proves the pipeline.

schemes.label: Falcon-512 (FN-DSA)
schemes.standardized: draft (FIPS 206); hash-to-point XOF swapped to BLAKE2s (non-standard)
schemes.offchain_prep: signer supplies mul hint (s1*h mod q; +29 sig felts)
schemes.notes: Smallest sig/key; demonstrated on Starknet (s2morrow). Hint-based verify (2 forward NTTs); BLAKE2s XOF keeps hash-to-point on-chain (message-binding) at corelib cost. Fragile signing; Cat-1.

schemes.label: Falcon-512 direct (no hint)
schemes.standardized: draft (FIPS 206); hash-to-point XOF swapped to BLAKE2s (non-standard)
schemes.notes: Textbook verification equation: s1*h computed on-chain as INTT(NTT(s1) . h_ntt) — 2 transforms because the pk is stored NTT-domain (a coefficient-domain pk needs 3 and busts the gas cap, cf. ericnordelo/pq-verifiers#1). Half the signature calldata of the hint variant; no signer-supplied hint in the trust surface.

schemes.label: Falcon-512 SHAKE-256 (standard)
schemes.offchain_prep: signer supplies mul hint (s1*h mod q; +29 sig felts)
schemes.notes: Standards-compliant hash-to-point: SHAKE-256 exactly per the Falcon spec, so signatures interoperate with any compliant signer (e.g. falcon.py) without a custom XOF. Same hint-based verify as falcon_512. On-chain cost is dominated by a pure-Cairo Keccak-f[1600] SHAKE-256; validation exceeds the step cap, so this is a benchmark target rather than a deployable account. A native keccak_f1600 syscall (SNIP-32) would collapse the hash cost and make it viable.

schemes.label: Falcon-512 Poseidon (native)
schemes.offchain_prep: signer supplies mul hint (s1*h mod q; +29 sig felts)
schemes.notes: Message-binding hash-to-point built on the native Poseidon builtin (hades_permutation squeeze), reusing the falcon_512 hint verify. On-chain cost is comparable to the BLAKE2s variant and ~5.5x cheaper than the standard SHAKE-256, and it fits the validation cap with wide margin. Being native field arithmetic it is also the most STARK-proving-friendly hash-to-point. Non-standard, so it needs a matching signer (a vendored Poseidon permutation in gen_falcon_fixture.py, checked against the corelib KAT).

schemes.label: ML-DSA-44 (Dilithium)
schemes.notes: Finalized; robust signing. Expected SHAKE-dominated cost in Cairo.

schemes.label: Poseidon-WOTS+ (hash-based)
schemes.notes: Cheapest Cairo compute; needs research-grade security analysis. Large signature.
